headline: How the inflation shockwave is spreading across Europe's economy
description: Eurozone inflation reached 3% in April, more than one percentage point higher since February. From gasoline to textiles, including petrochemicals and even condoms, most sectors have been affected.

headline: US-Iran ceasefire on brink as UAE reports attacks
Russia and Ukraine declare separate truces
'OPEC, founded on a desire for sovereignty, is now fracturing in the name of that same sovereignty'
description: The UAE's exit from the cartel of oil exporters exposes the exhaustion of collective logic as states grow increasingly determined to leverage their economic potential for their own geopolitical influence, writes Stéphane Lauer.

headline: How a dream cruise turned into a health nightmare
description: Hantavirus is responsible for the death of at least one of the three MV Hondius passengers who died this past month. While another three people on board are showing symptoms of respiratory distress, the luxury cruise ship, which Cape Verdean authorities have banned from docking, could head for the Canary Islands.

headline: Stars shine at the 2026 Met Gala despite Bezos co-chair controversy
description: The glitzy fashion event has drawn controversy after Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos and his wife were announced as lead sponsors and honorary co-chairs of the gala, also co-chaired by Beyoncé, Venus Williams and Nicole Kidman.

headline: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
description: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.

Hunt for methane leaks reveals persistent pollution around French oil and gas sites
French Interior Minister Nuñez: 'I have no problem with Islam in France. But I fight those who use it to undermine our Republic'
description: Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez shares his view on the relationship between Islam and the French Republic. He reveals the contents of a bill he is presenting, aimed at combating 'separatism' and 'entryism.'

headline: Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces fourth presidential run
description: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.

headline: Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
description: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.

headline: A European tax on oil superprofits is necessary
'In Afghanistan, as elsewhere, stories about drugs are also stories about power and society'
'Céline Dion will generate the equivalent of one-fifth of the economic impact of the Paris Olympics'
